content.text:
{
  "dimension": "methodology-fidelity",
  "score": 1,
  "rationale": "The coach uses almost no signature AoA language or frameworks, and the moves that are present run counter to the methodology for this concern. There are no signature questions from the AoA repertoire (no 'how is your anxiety right now,' no 'where do you feel that in your body,' no 'what are you defending,' no 'two words for fear,' no question about the texture of 'fine'). No frameworks are named or worked from (no VIEW, no fear-as-signal-of-care, no feel-over-figure, no body-based inquiry at all). Instead the coach uses generic insight-coaching: noticing verbal tells, hunting for the 'real thing,' and ultimately steering toward a father-as-bridge interpretation and a meaning-making question about identity. This is closer to a psychodynamic/insight-oriented frame than AoA — figuring rather than feeling, and an implicit teaching ('fear in someone like you probably doesn't look like fear') that argues the fearlessness rather than meeting it. The numbness layer, which is the live territory, is never approached as a felt texture; 'quiet' and 'flat' are treated as clues to decode rather than sensations to inhabit.",
  "evidence_turns": [2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12, 14],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Delivers a mini-teaching ('fear in someone like you probably doesn't look like fear') — argues the fearlessness rather than meeting it with 'okay.' No signature question; uses a generic imagine-success prompt instead of body/now inquiry."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Picks at the 'I think' qualifier and pivots to 'what's the next thing' — content-level investigation, no AoA move (no body, no texture, no welcoming of the flatness)."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Logical argument against the discipline explanation, then 'what does this launch represent for you' — insight-hunting frame, not AoA. No signature questions deployed."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Lands on 'it's quiet' — the exact numbness layer — but instead of inviting the client to feel the quiet, asks 'do you still believe in this?' Misses the clearest opportunity to use 'what does fine/quiet feel like' or move to the body."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "'When did it stop generating heat?' — a narrative/biographical question, not an AoA question. Pulls into story rather than present sensation."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "'Was your dad happy?' — a therapy-style probe into origin. Not from the AoA question set; moves further from the present-moment numbness."},
    {"turn": 14, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Offers an interpretation ('is steady what you actually are or what you became') — this is insight-coaching/reframing, not feel-over-figure. Caps a session that never touched the body or the felt texture of 'fine.'"}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": [],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["the-happiness-filter", "disassociating-in-the-coaching-room", "meet-resistance-with-okay", "lets-go-there", "fear-as-signal-of-care", "fear-in-body-vs-fear-in-head", "two-words-for-fear", "how-is-my-anxiety-right-now", "whats-the-difference-between-overcoming-and-feeling-fear", "disassociation-as-numbness-layer"],
  "notes": "Conversation is fluent and perceptive but methodologically generic-to-anti-AoA: argues the fearlessness implicitly, evangelizes an interpretation, and never goes to the body or the texture of 'fine.'"
}
